segments.text: After this, the Lord appointed seventy-two others and sent them two by two ahead of Him to every town and place He was about to visit.
And He told them,
The harvest is plentiful, but the workers are few. Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into His harvest.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Go! I am sending you out like lambs among wolves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Carry no purse or bag or sandals. Do not greet anyone along the road.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whatever house you enter, begin by saying, ‘Peace to this house.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If a man of peace is there, your peace will rest on him; if not, it will return to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Stay at the same house, eating and drinking whatever you are offered. For the worker is worthy of his wages. Do not move around from house to house.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If you enter a town and they welcome you, eat whatever is set before you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Heal the sick who are there and tell them, ‘The kingdom of God is near you.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But if you enter a town and they do not welcome you, go into the streets and declare,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘Even the dust of your town that clings to our feet, we wipe off as a testimony against you. Yet be sure of this: The kingdom of God is near.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you, it will be more bearable on that day for Sodom than for that town.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago, sitting in sackcloth and ashes.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment than for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever listens to you listens to Me; whoever rejects you rejects Me; and whoever rejects Me rejects the One who sent Me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The seventy-two returned with joy and said,
Lord, even the demons submit to us in Your name.
segments.speaker: The seventy-two returned with joy and

segments.text: So He told them,
I saw Satan fall like lightning from heaven.
segments.speaker: The seventy-two returned with joy and

segments.text: Behold, I have given you authority to tread on snakes and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy. Nothing will harm you.
segments.speaker: The seventy-two returned with joy and

segments.text: Nevertheless, do not rejoice that the spirits submit to you, but rejoice that your names are written in heaven.
segments.speaker: The seventy-two returned with joy and

segments.text: At that time Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and declared,

I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.
segments.speaker: The seventy-two returned with joy and

segments.text: All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows who the Son is except the Father, and no one knows who the Father is except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.
segments.speaker: The seventy-two returned with joy and

segments.text: Then Jesus turned to the disciples and said privately,
Blessed are the eyes that see what you see.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I tell you that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

text: The Parable of the Good Samaritan

segments.text: One day an expert in the law stood up to test Him.
Teacher,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he asked,
what must I do to inherit eternal life?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What is written in the Law?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
How do you read it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He answered,
‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength and with all your mind’ and ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You have answered correctly,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said.
Do this and you will live.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But wanting to justify himself, he asked Jesus,
And who is my neighbor?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus took up this question and said,
A man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho when he fell into the hands of robbers. They stripped him, beat him, and went away, leaving him half dead.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now by chance a priest was going down the same road, but when he saw him, he passed by on the other side.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So too, when a Levite came to that spot and saw him, he passed by on the other side.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But when a Samaritan on a journey came upon him, he looked at him and had compassion.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He went to him and bandaged his wounds, pouring on oil and wine. Then he put him on his own animal, brought him to an inn, and took care of him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The next day he took out two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper. ‘Take care of him,’ he said, ‘and on my return I will repay you for any additional expense.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which of these three do you think was a neighbor to the man who fell into the hands of robbers?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The one who showed him mercy,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
replied the expert in the law.
Then Jesus told him,
Go and do likewise.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As they traveled along, Jesus entered a village where a woman named Martha welcomed Him into her home.
She had a sister named Mary, who sat at the Lord’s feet listening to His message.
But Martha was distracted by all the preparations to be made. She came to Jesus and said,
Lord, do You not care that my sister has left me to serve alone? Tell her to help me!
segments.speaker: Martha

segments.text: Martha, Martha,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: the Lord replied,
you are worried and upset about many things.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the good portion, and it will not be taken away from her.
segments.speaker: the Lord
